Nexus Analytics // Mid Data Scientist

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) | Full-Time | $120,000 - $155,000 USD annually

Transforming business decisions through intelligent data

>> ABOUT

Nexus Analytics is a fast-growing data intelligence platform helping mid-market and
enterprise companies unlock actionable insights from their operational and customer data.

We build machine learning products and analytics infrastructure that empower non-technical
teams to make data-driven decisions at scale.

>> SUMMARY

We're seeking a Mid Data Scientist with 2-5 years of professional experience to join our
growing Analytics team. You'll work on designing and deploying machine learning models,

running A/B tests, and building statistical analyses that directly impact product decisions
and customer outcomes. You'll collaborate with product, engineering, and business

stakeholders to translate business questions into data insights and scalable solutions.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Design, develop, and validate machine learning models using scikit-learn, XGBoost, and

other frameworks to solve real-world business problems
- Conduct exploratory data analysis using Python (Pandas, NumPy) and SQL to understand

data patterns and generate hypotheses
- Plan, execute, and analyze A/B tests and experiments using statistical methods;

interpret results and communicate findings to stakeholders
- Build and maintain SQL queries to extract, transform, and aggregate data from

PostgreSQL and cloud data warehouses
- Collaborate with product and engineering teams to translate business requirements into

analytical specifications and model features
- Document code, methodologies, and findings in clear technical reports and Jupyter

notebooks for knowledge sharing
- Monitor model performance in production using MLflow and implement retraining pipelines

when drift is detected
- Mentor junior team members on statistical methods, best practices, and code quality

standards

>> MUST HAVE

- 2-5 years of professional experience as a Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, or

related quantitative role
- Strong proficiency in Python, including libraries like Pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn

for data manipulation and modeling
- Solid understanding of statistical methods, hypothesis testing, and experimental design

(A/B testing, power analysis)
- Experience writing and optimizing SQL queries to work with relational databases

(PostgreSQL, MySQL, or similar)

 - Demonstrated ability to build and evaluate machine learning models (regression,

classification, clustering) on real datasets
- Experience communicating technical findings to non-technical stakeholders

>> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with advanced ML frameworks (XGBoost, LightGBM, PyTorch) and ensemble
methods

- Familiarity with experiment tracking tools (MLflow, Weights & Biases) and versioning
strategies

- Experience with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) or
containerization (Docker)

>> STACK

Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) / SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL) / XGBoost / LightGBM / Jupyter

Notebooks / A/B Testing & Experimentation / Statistical Analysis & Hypothesis Testing / MLflow /

Git / Version Control / Tableau / Matplotlib / AWS / Cloud Platforms

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive salary and annual bonus (up to 15% of base)
- Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with company coverage for 90%

of premiums
- 401(k) matching program (up to 4% of salary)

- Flexible work arrangement with remote-first culture and 3 days in-office per week
- Professional development budget of $2,500 annually for courses, conferences, and

certifications
- Generous PTO policy: 20 days + 10 company holidays + unlimited sick leave

- Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 8 weeks for secondary)
- Equity grants (stock options) for all full-time employees

>> APPLY

Apply on our careers page at www.nexusanalytics.com/careers or send your resume and a brief
cover letter to careers@nexusanalytics.com. Include a link to your GitHub profile or

portfolio showcasing recent data science projects. We review applications on a rolling
basis.
